name: PET
notes: Polyethylene terephthalate; common for water/soda bottles. Brittle below glass transition (~70°C); creep under sustained load.

name: PCR-PET
notes: Post-consumer recycled PET (rPET). Mechanical recycling cuts embodied energy ~79%, so cradle-to-gate GHG is roughly a quarter of virgin PET. Mechanical properties within ~5% of virgin bottle-grade for fully washed flake.

name: PCR-PET-50
notes: 50/50 virgin-PCR blend. Compromise between performance and footprint; typical first step before going to 100% PCR.

name: HDPE
notes: High-density polyethylene; good chemical resistance; ESCR depends on grade. Tougher than PET in cold drops.

name: PCR-HDPE
notes: Post-consumer recycled HDPE. Mechanical recycling reduces embodied energy ~88%. Stiffness and strength within ~6% of virgin. Colourants may darken; usually acceptable for back-of-pack and HPC packaging.

name: PP
notes: Polypropylene; good fatigue performance, brittle near 0°C. Common for caps and crates.

name: PCR-PP
notes: Post-consumer recycled PP. Embodied energy ~79–88% lower than virgin. Slight reduction in stiffness and impact at 0°C — validate cold-chamber drop for cap/closure use.

name: LDPE
notes: Soft, flexible; squeeze bottles, films. Low stiffness limits stack strength.

name: PVC
notes: Rigid PVC; clear bottles for non-carbonated products.

name: PS
notes: Polystyrene; brittle. Drop and handling risk should be biased upward in surrogate analysis.

name: Glass
notes: Brittle. Surface flaws dominate strength; thermal shock and edge contact are primary failure modes. Container glass typically already contains ~30% cullet.

name: Aluminium
notes: Internal pressure resists denting; vulnerable to point loads on rim.

name: PCR-Aluminium
notes: Aluminium can stock with 75% PCR. Secondary aluminium needs ~5% of the energy of primary smelting — the single largest carbon lever for canned packs.

name: Corrugated B-flute
notes: Edge crush test (ECT) controls compression. Humidity can reduce strength by 30–50%. Most board already runs ~60% recycled fibre.

name: Corrugated E-flute
notes: Fine single-wall flute (~1.5 mm). Low caliper gives a smooth print face and tight stacking, but lower ECT and cushioning than B/C — best for retail-ready and folding-style boxes. Humidity can reduce strength by 30–50%.

name: Corrugated C-flute
notes: Taller single-wall flute (~4 mm). Higher ECT and cushioning than B/E — the default for general shipping cases and palletised loads. Humidity can reduce strength by 30–50%.

name: Kraft Paperboard
notes: Folding cartons; grain direction matters for crease strength.

name: PCR-Corrugated
notes: High-recycled-content corrugated board (90% OCC fibre). Cradle-to-gate carbon ~37% lower than standard 60% recycled board. ECT within 5% of virgin-fibre equivalent when conditioned at 23°C/50% RH; strength degrades faster in high-humidity environments.

name: PCR-Corrugated-E
notes: High-recycled-content E-flute board (90% OCC fibre). Cradle-to-gate carbon ~37% lower than standard 60% recycled board. Fine flute keeps a smooth print face; ECT within ~5% of virgin-fibre equivalent at 23°C/50% RH; strength degrades faster in high humidity.

name: PCR-Corrugated-C
notes: High-recycled-content C-flute board (90% OCC fibre). Cradle-to-gate carbon ~37% lower than standard 60% recycled board. Taller flute retains the cushioning and stack strength of virgin C-flute within ~5% when conditioned at 23°C/50% RH; strength degrades faster in high humidity.

name: PCR-Kraft-Paperboard
notes: Recycled-content coated recycled board (CRB/GRB) for backer cards and folding cartons. 80% post-consumer fibre; cradle-to-gate carbon ~50% lower than virgin SBS. Stiffness reduced ~7% — validate crease and hanger-hole tear strength before specifying.

name: PCR-LDPE
notes: Mechanically recycled LDPE for flexible film sealant layers. Carbon intensity ~74% lower than virgin LDPE (2.10 kg CO₂e/kg). Slight reduction in seal strength and optical clarity; validate heat-seal window and peel force before specifying for food-contact laminates.
